findings.title: Global setup backups cannot be restored by the existing CLI
findings.rationale: Global setup writes its backup manifest with repo_root set to the user Codex home, but restore/uninstall still require the manifest repo_root to canonicalize to the active project repo_root. That means the new user-scope backup created by codex1 setup cannot be consumed by the existing restore or uninstall flows, so the backup is not actually reversible through the current CLI surface.
findings.suggested_next_action: Add a user-scope restore/uninstall path that keys off CODEX_HOME/HOME for global setup backups, or change the manifest/command contract so the new backups are discoverable and restorable by an explicit user-level recovery command.

findings.title: Human-readable global setup output hides the actual mutations
findings.rationale: The non-JSON renderer for global setup always prints `changed paths: none` even when the command just mutated config.toml or hooks.json. That makes the default human-facing completion report inaccurate and hides the actual surface that changed.
findings.suggested_next_action: Render the actual changed_paths list, or at minimum include the number and names of changed user-scope files in the human report so the completion summary matches the mutation that occurred.
